sect1.title: Go-&go-version;
sect1.sect2:
Introduction to Go
The Go package contains a compiler for the Go language, a Google-supported, object-oriented programming language.
Modern Go is written in the Go language, thus requiring a Go compiler to compile the new compiler. If Go isn't installed already, then the instructions below will have you compile multiple older versions of Go, starting with one that was written in C, and the instructions compile newer versions from that point.
&long-build-time;
Download:
Go Dependencies
Required (to download older versions of Go)
wget

sect2.title: Bootstrapping Go
sect2.para: If you haven't installed Go yet, you will need to bootstrap it. GCC-Go is unsuitable as it has several issues that prevent it from being able to build Go properly. This process will build an older C version, followed by newer versions which are written fully in Go.

note.para: Don't do the process in the main extracted directory. This will result in numerous file conflicts later in the process.

sect2.para: First create a list of previous Go versions that must be downloaded and built:
Using Wget, download all the Go versions that will be used to bootstrap modern Go:
Start a subshell that will exit on error:
Now bootstrap all the Go versions:

screen.userinput:
for go in $(grep -v '^#' go-bootstrap-&go-version;) do go=$(basename "$go") godir=${go%.tar.?z*} echo "Bootstrapping $godir"
rm -rf $godir tar -xf $go pushd $godir
if [ -e src/cmd/dist/a.h ]; then sed -i '/typedef int bool/d' src/cmd/dist/a.h fi
cd src CFLAGS+=" -std=gnu17" sh make.bash -v popd export GOROOT_BOOTSTRAP=$PWD/$godir done; exit

sect2.title: Installation of Go
sect2.para: As the &root; user, create the directory that all the Go components will be installed in:
In order to build modern Go, ensure you already have Go installed or bootstrapped Go.
If you bootstrapped Go, extract the main Go download and change into the extracted directory, then set a variable telling the build system where to find the bootstrapped build:
Install Go by running the following commands:

screen.userinput: cd src && CGO_ENABLED=1 GOROOT_FINAL=/opt/go-&go-version; sh make.bash -v

ln -sfvn go-&go-version; /opt/go && for i in ../{bin,pkg,src,lib,misc,api,test}; do cp -va $i /opt/go-&go-version; done && ln -svf /opt/go/bin/go /usr/bin && ln -svf /opt/go/bin/gofmt /usr/bin && install -vDm644 ../go.env \ -t /opt/go-&go-version;/ &&
rm -rf /opt/go-&go-version;/pkg/bootstrap && rm -rf /opt/go-&go-version;/pkg/obj/go-build && rm -rf /usr/share/doc/go-&go-version; && cp -va ../doc /usr/share/doc/go-&go-version;
